titlestmt.titleproper: James H. Etheridge and family collection
titlestmt.author: Toby A. Appel

publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday December 5, 2018 at 03:00

did.unittitle: James H. Etheridge and family collection

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: James Henry Etheridge, gynecologist, was born in St. Johnsville, New York. March 20, 1844. Son of a physician, Francis B. Etheridge, he studied medicine with his father before entering Rush Medical College, Chicago, in 1866. After graduation in 1869, he began a general practice in Evanston, Illinois. In 1870, he married Harriet Elizabeth Powers, of Evanston, daughter of wealthy businessman Heman G. Powers. They traveled abroad and and he visited hospitals in London. On this return he set up practice in Chicago in 1871 and became part of the teaching faculty of Rush Medical College. At first, he was a successful general practitioner, and then he specialized in gynecology. In 1889, he became Professor of Gynecology at Rush Medical College, and in 1892, Professor of Obstetrics and Gynecology. He was active in medical organizations and served as President of the Chicago Medical Society and the Chicago Gynecological Society. At the height of his career, he died suddenly on February 9, 1899. He was known as a man of good nature and wit, and as a skillful and rapid operator.

did.unittitle: Biographical information /Obituaries
Correspondence of Etheridge Grant on the disposal of J.H. Etheridge's Personal effects

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Etheridge Grant was a descendent of J.H. and Harriet (Hattie) Etheridge. Their daughter Fanny married John MacGregor Grant.

did.unittitle: Handwritten obituary on Rush Medical College stationary
Resolutions of the Senior Class of Rush Medical college on the death of Prof. Etheridge

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains the handwritten Resulotions as sent tyo Etheridge's family and an issue The Corpuscle (April, 1899), the alumni journal of Rush Medical College with the printed resolution.

did.unittitle: Obituary: Issue of The American Gynaecological and Obstetrical Journal
Obituary: Issue of the Chicago Medical Recorder
Obituary: issue of The Corpuscle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Corpuscle was the "Official Organ of tghe Alumni Association of Rush Medical College, Chicago, Ill." The article, "James Henry Etheridge, A.M., M.D.: In Memoriam" (volume 8, pp. 273-276 and portrait) was presented to the faculty by Prof. James B. Herrick.

did.unittitle: Obituary: Issue of Journal of the American Medical Association
Obituary: Issue of Journal of Scientific Medicine
Obituary: Issue of The Medical Dial
Obituary: Issue of the Revue de gynecologie et de chirurgie abdominale
Obituary: Issue of the Tri-State Medical Journal and Practitioner
Obituary: Issue of The Chicago Daily Tribune
Obituary: Issue of the Chicago Evening Post
Obituary: Issue of The Chicago Times-Herald
Other obituaries

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper clippings and printed report, "Monthly Meetings of the Alumnae Association of the Illinois Training School for Nursdes, Febrary 2, 1899 [sic] cobntaining an obituary of Etheridge on page 8. For ten years Etheridge had been lecturer and physician to the School.

did.unittitle: Caricature of Etheridge and poem on minced pie, by C.J. P. U..
Other publications that mention Etheridge
The Presbyterian Hospital of the City of Chicago, Bulletin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a history of the hospital that mentions J. H. Etheridge.

did.unittitle: James H. Etheridge - photographs
Correspondence of James H. Etheridge

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains only correspondence from 1894 to 1899. The files contain mainly letters received, although there is an occasional draft of a letter by Etheridge. ASmong his corresoppndents are a number of women patients who were operated on by Etheridge. Also in the collection, but not in this series, are carbons of typed correspondence sent by Etheridge.
The correspondence was housed in two boxes of Amberg's Popular Letter File sold by Amberg File & Index Co., Chicago. Many of the leters were not acurately filed under the proper letter in the index.
Soomeone, possibly, Mrs. Etheridge who lived into the 1920s, annotated a nunber of the letters.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, A
Correspondence: "Barkee" and "Bar and Kee" (a relative)
Correspondence, B
Correspondence, C
Correspondence, D
Correspondence: Etheridge, Annie and Locke (sister-in-law? and brother)

Correspondence: Harper, William R., President of University of Chicago
Correspondence: Henrotin, Fernand
Correspondence: Hyde, James Nevins
Correspondence, H
Correspondence: Ingals, E. Fletcher
Correspondence, J
Correspondence, K
Correspondence: Lea Brothers & Co., Phliadelphia
Correspondence, M
Correspondence, N
Correspondence, O
Correspondence: Park, Roswell
Correspondence: Powers, Heman R., brother-in-law

odd.head: General
odd.p: See also Moline Malleable Iron Co. folder under Oversize. There is an obituary of Heman R. POwers in Box Folder .

did.unittitle: Correspondence: Powers, Heman R., brother-in-law
Correspondence: Pryor, W. R., nephew
Correspondence, P
Correspondence, R
Correspondence: Senn, N.

odd.head: General
odd.p: The correspondent is not Etheridge's colleague Nicholas Senn, but rather a younger man going into federal service.

did.unittitle: Correspondence: Stone, E. M. and Mary R.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: R. M. Stone, M.D. was a physician in Omaha, Nebraska and his wife, Mary, was a patient of Etheridge. The case was discussed in detail.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, S
Correspondence, T.
Correspondence, V
Correspondence, W
Correspondence: "Alice," a patient
Correspondence: Unidentified
Correspondence concerning calcium carbide in operations for cancer of the cervix

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the issue of Journal of the American Medical Association for July 2, 1898, Etheridge had published "A Note on the Treatmenr of Carcinoma of the Uterus," in which he asked for information from others on treatment by calcium carbide. These respondesn from physicians were grouped by Etheridge.

did.unittitle: Correspondence: "Military"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These materials were grouped by Etheridge. He had served in the Civil War and had later served in the First Brigade, Illinois National Guard.

did.unittitle: Correspondence: "Military"
Carbons of letters by James H. Etheridge

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound volume of carbon copies of typed letters by Etheridge arranged chronologically, September 20, 1897-December 5, 1898. Volume contains 1004 numbered pages plus an index at the beginning of the volume. See next folder.

did.unittitle: Carbons of letters by James H. Etheridge

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder containing unbound carbon copies of letters from December 5, 1898 to February 9, 1899, the day of his death. Carbons are paged from 1 to 103.

did.unittitle: Powers/Throop family correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is correspondence from Harriet (Hattie) Powers Etheridge's side of the family, collected by Hattie Etheridge and her heirs. The letters were stored in several boxes of Amberg's Patent Indexes. Hattie Etheridge was the daughter of Heman G. Powers and Sarah (Throop) Powers. The bulk of this collection consists of letters to Hattie Etheridge's mother and father, Sarah Throop Powers and Heman G. Powers. Heman G. Powers's correspondence includes material on his various businesses. The collection also contains some letters by and to Hattie Etheridge, her children, and other relatives and friends.

did.unittitle: Correspondence of Harriet (Hattie) Powers Etheridge and children

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Harriet (Hattie) Elizabeth Powers was the daughter of Heman G. Powers and Sarah (Throop) Powers. She ws born in 1848 and married James H. Etheridge on June 22, 1870. She was still living in 1922. Her daughters were Fanny Etheridge (Grant) and Emily (or Emma) Etheridge (Brewer). As of 1920, she was living with Emily Brewer and Emily's husband William Brewer in Chicago.

did.unittitle: Hattie Elizabeth Powers to parents, Sarah Throop and Heman G. Powers
Hattie Powers Etheridge to parents, Sarah Throop and Heman G. Powers
Hattie Powers Etheridge to parents, Sarah Throop and Heman G. Powers
Hattie E. Powers to grandmother, Sarah Fales Throop
George Throop and Sarah Fales Throop (grandparents) to Hattie E. Powers
Letters to Hattie Powers on the death of Sarah Fales Throop (grandmother)
Heman G. Powers (father) to Hattie E. Powers
William (Will) Brewer (son-in-law) to Hattie Etheridge

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Brewer married Hattie Etheridge's daughter Emily. He served in FRance in World War I.

did.unittitle: Elizabeth Duane to Hattie Powers Etheridge
James H. Etheridge, telegram to Hattie Powers Etheridge
Mary Powers Hicks to Hattie Powers Etheridge
Fanny ("Billy") Etheridge to grandparents, Heman and Sarah Powers
Emily E. Brewer to Fanny Etheridge
Clinton Furbish to "Billy" (Fanny Etheridge)
Mary Albin Throop to Fanny Etheridge
Ruthven Pike to Fanny Etheridge Grant
Correspondence of Emily Etheridge with grandparents, Heman and Sarah Powers
Letters to/from Heman G. and Sarah E. Throop Powers
Emily E. Brewer, Keeseville, N.Y., to cousins Sarah and Heman
Emily E. Brewer, Keeseville, N.Y., to Heman G. and Sarah Powers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some of the letters run to multuiple numbered pages. Pages may be out of order and not linked with the correct beginning of the letter.

did.unittitle: Mary Burtt, Kalamazoo, Mich., to Sarah Powers
Munro Burtt, Kalamazoo, Mich., to Heman G. Powers
Heman G. Powers to Molly Rogers Calhoun
George and Mary Chahoon, Ausable Falls, N.Y., to Heman and Sarah Powers

odd.head: General
odd.p: They were associated with J. & J. Rogers Iron Company.

did.unittitle: W. F. Coolbaugh, Chicago, to Heman G. Powers

odd.head: General
odd.p: W. F. Coolbaugh was president of the Union National Bank, Chicago.

did.unittitle: William W. Fairwell, Chicago, to Heman G. Powers
Chas. H. Foote, Ausable Forks, N.Y., to Heman G. Powers
Mary P. Frazer to Heman G. and Sarah Powers
Clinton Furbish, Brooklyn, N.Y., to Heman G. Powers
H. D. Graves, Ausable Forks, N.Y., to Heman G. Powers

odd.head: General
odd.p: H. D. Graves and Kate Rogers Graves (next folder) were associated with the J. & J. Rogers Iron Co.

did.unittitle: Kate Rogers Graves to Heman G. and Sarah Powers

odd.head: General
odd.p: Kate Rogers Graves was a close relative of Heman G. Powers. In the correspondence, she was Kate Rogers until the early 1860s when she married H. D. Graves. See also Mary Rogers.

did.unittitle: Henry Green to Heman G. Powers

odd.head: General
odd.p: Green was possibly Heman G. Powers' brother-in-law. His sister Elizabeth married a Henry Green.

did.unittitle: Mary Albin Throop Hallock to Sarah Throop Powers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mary Albin Throop was Sarah's sister. Later letters were written from Saranac Lake, N.Y.

did.unittitle: W. W. Hartwell, Plattsburgh, N.Y., to Heman G. Powers
Caroline Keeler, Keeseville, N.Y., to Sarah and Heman Powers
Sarah E. Keeler, Keeseville, N.Y., to "Cousin Sarah" [Sarah Powers]
Mrs. M. A. Keise [or Kuse] to Heman Powers
H. F. Leavitt, Middlebury Female Seminary, to Heman G. Powers
John F. McGuire, Clinton, Iowa, to Heman G. Powers
Frank H. Palmer to Heman G. Powers
Julia and W. B. Parkinson, Oshkosh, Wisconsin, to Heman G. Powers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Julia Parkinson called Heman G. Powers "Uncle Heman."

did.unittitle: Ruthven W. Pike to Heman G. Powers
Emily Church Powers to parents, Sarah Throop and Heman G. Powers

odd.head: General
odd.p: Emily Church Powers was born in 1847.

did.unittitle: Heman G. Powers to Sarah Powers (wife)
Major Powers (father) to Heman G. Powers
Sarah Fales Powers to parents, Heman G.and Sarah Powers

odd.head: General
odd.p: Sarah Fales Powers (1865-1924) was the youngest child of Heman G. and Sarah Throop Powers.

did.unittitle: J. C. C. Reynolds, Keeseville, N.Y., to Heman G. Powers
James Rogers, Ausable Forks, N.Y., to Heman G. Powers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rogers was associated with the J. & J. Rogers Iron Company. The last item concerns the estate of James Rogers. See also Kate Rogers Graves.

did.unittitle: Mary Rogers, Ausable Forks, N.Y., to Heman G. Powers
Heman G. Powers to Mssrs. Rogers, Martin & Co., Ausable Forks, N.Y.
L. B. Sidway to Heman G. Powers
Roswell Skeel to Heman G. Powers
J. D. Smith, Vergennes, Vermont, to Heman G. Powers
George Throop (father-in-law) to Heman G. Powers

odd.head: General
odd.p: George Throop was the father of Sarah Throop POwers, wife of Heman G. Powers.

did.unittitle: Lucia Throop to Sarah Throop Powers
Sarah Fales Throop (mother) and George Throop (father) to Sarah Throop Powers
Harriet Powers Watson and Thomas Watson and family to Heman G. and Sarah Powers
C. T. Wheeler, Chicago, to Heman G. Powers
Letters to Heman G. Powers from abroad, unidentified writers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These were grouped together by Harriet Etheridge or other family member.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous letters to Heman G. Powers
Miscellaneous letters to Sarah Powers
Documents on the funeral of Major Powers, father of Heman G. Powers
Heman G. Powers, Receipts from abroad
Heman G. Powers, Bonds, agreements, and other legal papers
Supreme Court of Illinois: Henry W. Kingsbury vs. Heman G. Powers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed pamphlet, "Appellee's closing Argument on Rehearing,"; Finding of the Spreme Court. Ruthven W. Pike served as attorney for Powers.

did.unittitle: Estate of Jeremiah B. Briggs, Heman G. Powers Administrator
Other family correspondence

Heman R.. Powers, miscellaneous correspondence
Suzie F. Powers to parents
"Mrs. Currier" to "Uncle Dudley"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One letter has been annotated "Mrs. Frank Palmer."

did.unittitle: Clinton Furbish to "Fred"
J. H. Etheridge writings
Articles/Reprints
Articles/Reprints
Articles/Reprints
Articles/Reprints
"Menstruation," typescript
Manuscript draft obituary of Willaim Wright Jaggard (1857-1896)
Presentation on fever
Organizations and societies
Rush Medical College, Annual Announcements

title.part: The Corpuscle, edited by the Students of Rush Medical College

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five issues. Issues contain references to faculty including ti Etheridge.

did.unittitle: Rush Medical College ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a new year's card, clippings, and a small enhraving of Rush Medical College. Two large photographs, one of Rush Medical College, and the other of Etheridge opearing are in the oversize box.

did.unittitle: American Medical Association Annual Meeting, Denver, ephemera
American Medical Association Annual Meeting, St. Louis, ephemera
Congress of American Physicians and Surgeons, Washington, D.C,.
Ninth International Medical Congress, Washington, D.C.
Obstetrical and gynecological societies - ephemera
State and local medical Societies ephemera
Annual Report, Illinois Training School for Nurses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Etheridge was a member of the Advisory Board.

did.unittitle: Other organizations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Among the items is an 1895 "Private Report from the Physicians' Business Bureau' listing of people with poor credit.

did.unittitle: Medical practice
Case histories

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In this bound volume (33x21 cm), Eheridge wrote a full account of his cases and treatment. The volume begins with a few pages on notes on a course of anatomy and physiology he attended in Ann Arbor, Michigan, in 1866. Some docuemnts are inserted in the volume, including a letter and case history of Mrs. Hubbard sent by Dr. E. L. Homes, March 10, 1880

did.unittitle: Patient ledger

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound volume (31x14 cm) lists patients by day, chargesaand payments. There is an index to names at the beginning of the volume. The book had little information on diagnoses and treatments.

did.unittitle: Account of travel to London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound volume (24x20 cm) with an account of his experiences in London. Etheridge married in 1870. He noted that they had spent the months of September through the most of November visiting Paris, Switzerland, Italy, Austria, Bavaria, Wurtemberg, Saxony, Russia, the Rhine and Belgium, and had now (November 26) arrived in London. "Our chief on\bject here is to benefit me, by walking the hospitalas & gaining all the information I can here elicit, professionally. The chief object of this book is [to] note down things aaf interest..." Most of the remainde4r of thebook lists menus and recipes.

did.unittitle: St. Vincent Hall prescriptions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Possibly St. Vincent Hall was a dispensary. The bound volume in pencil (20x16 cm) gives the date, the name of the patient, and the prescripion.

did.unittitle: Patient ledger

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Large bound volume (37x23cm) containing an index of names, and a list of patients, dates of visits, amounts billed, and amounts paid.

did.unittitle: Cash book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This bound volume (22x14 cm.) stamped "Cash" in goldleaf on the front cover, has been organized by month. For each month, Etheridge listed several entries for "Office" followed by the names of various people, and amounts of money.

did.unittitle: Record of patients and operations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound pre-printed volume (23x15 cm.) with "Dr. Etheridge" in gold leaf on the cover. There is no indication if there was a publisher. The open pages have columns for date, name of patient, name of [refering] physician, fees, operation, arrival, and remarks. Only a small poertion of the volume has been used.

did.unittitle: Cases numbered 2019-2862

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The bound volume is a pre-printed case book for diseases of women. It has spaceas for name, date, age, whether married, number of childreb, number of miscarriages, general appeaernce and conditiion, family history, symptons (broken down by type), menstruation,... diagnosis, and treatment. There is no publishing information for this pre-printed book. Etheridge enetered an index at the begninning. There is a pasted pice on the cover which syas, Mr. H. L. Humphrey, Masseuse, 4943 Woodlawn Ave."

did.unittitle: Oversize box: Additional patient records, correspondence.and iscellany
Correspondence of J. H. Etheridge: Notaway Mining Company, Cripple Creek, Colorado
Map of a mining site
Correspondence of Heman G. Powers: Durands and Powers, Wholesale Grocers
J. H. Etheridge, presentation, "The Benefits of Association "
"Names of patients treated By Dr. Etheridge in St. Joseph's Hospital, since June 1st, 1872."
Patient receipts, money owed, and paid
J. H. Etheridge, Notes on a case of extra-uterine pregnancy
J. H. Etheridge, Notes on ovulation
Flat box: Large photographs, certificates and diplomas
Large photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph of "J.H. Etheridge's Clinic," showing an Etheridge opeating in an mphitheater of spectators; photograoph of Rush Medical College; photograph of an operation by Etheridge with the team standing around the patient on a gurney, ca. 1895.

did.unittitle: Francis B. Etheridge, J. H. Etheridge's father
Membership in the Medical Society of the County of Montgomery
License to practice medicine from the "Medical Society of the County of Herkimer and State of New-York"
Doctorate in medicine from the University City of New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Large vellum diploma.

did.unittitle: James H. Etheridge
Certificate for a course in chemistry taken by J.H. Etheridge at the University of Michigan
Medical diploma from Rush Medical College
Master's in Literary Arts, University of Chicago
Certificate of original membership in the Chicago Gynaecological Society
Certificate as a Charter Member of the Chicago Medico-Legal Society
Resolutions of Illinois Training School for Nurses on the death of James H. Etheridge
Memorial from the Physicians Club on the death of James H. Etheridge
